content_en:
The Wafayat al-A'yan achieved canonical status in Arabic biographical literature with unusual speed. Within decades of its composition, it was being cited by scholars throughout the Islamic world as the standard reference for information about distinguished figures of Islamic history. Its combination of reliable information, literary quality, and broad cultural scope made it indispensable to scholars in numerous fields.

The work stimulated a tradition of continuation and supplement. Khalil ibn Aybak as-Safadi, one of the greatest biographers of the subsequent generation, wrote his enormous Wafi bil-Wafayat explicitly as a continuation and expansion of Ibn Khallikan's work — a project that eventually grew to thirty volumes. The fact that as-Safadi felt it appropriate to continue rather than replace the Wafayat speaks to its foundational status. Similarly, later scholars who wrote biographical dictionaries frequently structured their works to complement the Wafayat by covering categories or periods it had omitted.

European orientalists encountered the Wafayat early in their engagement with Arabic literature and recognized its extraordinary value as a historical source. William MacGuckin de Slane produced a complete French translation of the work in four volumes (1843–1871), accompanied by detailed notes — a monument of nineteenth-century orientalism that brought the Wafayat to the attention of Western scholars who lacked Arabic. This translation was widely used by historians of the medieval Islamic world for over a century and remains useful despite its age.

For historians of Arabic literature, the Wafayat has been one of the primary sources for understanding the lives and contexts of major poets, and the extensive verse quotations it contains have served as important textual witnesses for editing classical Arabic poetry. For historians of Islamic medicine, philosophy, and science, Ibn Khallikan's entries on physicians, philosophers, and scientists provide essential biographical data. For political historians, the entries on caliphs, viziers, and military commanders contain information about governance and politics unavailable in most other sources.

Modern scholars have also used the Wafayat for prosopographical analysis — systematic study of social patterns through analysis of biographical data across large numbers of individuals. The work's information about scholars' geographical origins, educational trajectories, patronage relationships, and career paths provides material for understanding the social infrastructure of Islamic learning.

Critiques of the work have generally been limited. Some scholars have noted occasional errors in dates or attribution, and the alphabetical rather than chronological arrangement means that understanding historical development requires cross-referencing numerous entries. But these are minor limitations given the work's extraordinary scope and quality.
content_id:
Wafayat al-A'yan mencapai status kanonik dalam literatur biografi Arab dengan kecepatan yang tidak biasa. Dalam beberapa dekade setelah penyusunannya, karya ini sudah dikutip oleh para ulama di seluruh dunia Islam sebagai referensi standar untuk informasi tentang tokoh-tokoh terkemuka sejarah Islam. Perpaduan antara informasi yang andal, kualitas sastra, dan cakupan budaya yang luas menjadikannya tidak tergantikan bagi para ulama di berbagai bidang.

Karya ini merangsang tradisi kelanjutan dan suplemen. Khalil ibn Aybak as-Safadi, salah satu ahli biografi terbesar dari generasi berikutnya, menulis Al-Wafi bil-Wafayat yang sangat besar secara eksplisit sebagai kelanjutan dan perluasan karya Ibn Khallikan — sebuah proyek yang akhirnya berkembang menjadi tiga puluh jilid. Kenyataan bahwa as-Safadi merasa tepat untuk melanjutkan daripada mengganti Wafayat berbicara tentang status pondasinya. Demikian pula, para ulama kemudian yang menulis kamus biografi sering kali menyusun karya mereka untuk melengkapi Wafayat dengan mencakup kategori-kategori atau periode-periode yang telah dihilangkan.

Para orientalis Eropa menemukan Wafayat di awal keterlibatan mereka dengan sastra Arab dan mengakui nilainya yang luar biasa sebagai sumber historis. William MacGuckin de Slane menghasilkan terjemahan Prancis lengkap dari karya tersebut dalam empat jilid (1843–1871), disertai catatan terperinci — sebuah monumen orientalisme abad kesembilan belas yang membawa Wafayat ke perhatian para ulama Barat yang tidak menguasai bahasa Arab. Terjemahan ini banyak digunakan dan membuka jalan bagi pengakuan global atas nilai keilmuan karya tersebut.

Dalam keilmuan Arab modern, Wafayat telah berulang kali diedit dan diterbitkan. Edisi kritis Ihsan Abbas dalam delapan jilid (Beirut, 1968–1972) menjadi standar dan dikutip secara universal dalam keilmuan tentang peradaban Islam klasik.
